articles.title: My Son
articles.author: Jane Howard
articles.complete_text:
My son, I cannot think of thee
As lying dead beneath the sod.
Thy spirit lives, thou journeys far
Into the paradise of God.

My son, I can but think of thee
As happy, pure, and undefiled.
As in that better place thou art,
Because of this, I'm reconciled.

My son, I want to live, that when
My life has past mortality,
By faith and with good works, I'll come
To paradise and dwell with thee.

Praise God, the Father and the Son,
That by and through his holy grace,
We live for all eternity—
If faithful, see him face to face.
articles.notes: This is a complete poem by Jane Howard that appears to be standalone content, not part of advertisements or misc content. It's a religious poem about loss and eternal life that fits the magazine's content.

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of advertisements for LDS garments, Columbia Grafonolas, Royal Bread, various Salt Lake City businesses, the General Board member listing, table of contents, advertisers' directory, and back matter with subscription information and more advertisements. There's also a partial fragment of the beginning of an article about Relief Society nursing training that appears to continue elsewhere.
